full_text_rationale: Full text proposes Reasoning Memory, an external procedural-knowledge datastore distilled from prior reasoning trajectories and retrieved at test time, with quantitative evaluation across math, science, and coding benchmarks including LiveCodeBench.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 012; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Di Wu, Devendra Singh Sachan, Wen-tau Yih, Mingda Chen
titulo: Procedural Knowledge at Scale Improves Reasoning
formato_armazenamento: Reasoning Memory datastore of approximately 32 million compact procedural entries, each pairing a self-contained subquestion with a concise subroutine distilled from prior step-by-step reasoning trajectories.
framework_agente: Reasoning Memory retrieval-augmented reasoning framework with trajectory decomposition, self-verbalized in-thought query generation, dense retrieval, and test-time scaling over retrieved subroutines
llm_utilizado: DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Llama-8B, OpenThinker3-7B, Qwen3-32B, QwQ-32B, Qwen3-8B, ReasonIR-8B retriever
metodo_avaliacao: benchmark; retrieval-baseline comparison; compute-matched test-time scaling comparison; inference-budget scaling; datastore size and composition ablation; datastore/query-design ablation; qualitative failure analysis
benchmarks_utilizados: AIME 2024, AIME 2025, MATH500, GPQA-Diamond, LiveCodeBench V1-4, LiveCodeBench V5-6
metricas_desempenho: Accuracy, execution-based Pass@1 for LiveCodeBench, sample budget m, retained sample budget n, statistical significance markers
metricas_cl: Datastore size and composition effects, cross-domain procedural-transfer analysis, subquestion decomposition ablation, self-verbalized query ablation, inference-budget scaling to m=100
baseline_comparado: No RAG, Trajectory RAG prefix, Trajectory RAG summary, Template RAG ReasonFlux, Template RAG Self-Discover, Document RAG CompactDS, Document RAG Google, retrieval-free Length Scaling
tokens_saida_por_tarefa: Maximum output length is set to 32,768 tokens in the implementation; per-task input/output token means are not reported.
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: Final datastore contains approximately 32 million subquestion-subroutine pairs; average subquestions contain 19.2 tokens, average subroutines contain 207.9 tokens, and each source trajectory contributes 10.5 subquestions.
custo_por_tarefa: NR monetary; inference cost is reported through sample budgets such as m=8, m=30, and scaling analyses up to m=100.
resultado_principal: Across math, science, and coding benchmarks, Reasoning Memory improves over no retrieval by up to 19.2% and over the strongest compute-matched baseline by 7.9%.
desempenho_vs_baseline: On LiveCodeBench V1-4 with Qwen3-32B and m=30, Reasoning Memory reaches 0.471 versus Length Scaling 0.405 and No RAG 0.387 at m=8; on LiveCodeBench V5-6 it reaches 0.508 versus Length Scaling 0.476 and No RAG 0.465.
custo_vs_baseline: The method can outperform retrieval-free Length Scaling at matched sample budgets, but the paper does not provide monetary or end-to-end token-cost comparisons.
limitacoes: Document retrieval can degrade reasoning models; the method can fail when self-verbalized queries become over-specific or when retrieved procedures mismatch the problem setup; benefits vary after similar corpus knowledge is internalized by model training.
ameacas_validade: Results depend on Nemotron-derived trajectory coverage, decomposition prompt quality, ReasonIR retrieval, benchmark contamination assumptions, sample-budget choices, and whether math/science/coding reasoning transfers to agentic software-engineering workflows.
